response.justification: Mohammed brings 17+ years of broad engineering experience with a strong fullstack foundation in TypeScript, Node.js, React, and cloud tooling (AWS CDK, Docker, Kubernetes), directly matching the primary stack. His current role at re:cinq explicitly covers AI-agent infrastructure and MCP framework development, satisfying the increasingly critical AI agent requirement. The main gaps are the absence of NestJS and explicit Vitest/Playwright experience, though his deeply ingrained TDD culture and authorship of custom testing tools suggest he can close those gaps quickly.
strengths: 17+ years of polyglot full-stack experience with direct TypeScript/Node.js/React production use across multiple companies
AI agent and MCP infrastructure work at re:cinq directly satisfies the rare AI agent development requirement
Deeply ingrained TDD culture evidenced by conference talks, workshops, and authoring a D-language test runner (Trial) and fluent-asserts library
concerns: NestJS is not mentioned anywhere in the resume, yet it is the primary backend framework for this role
Jest, Vitest, and Playwright are not explicitly listed; testing framework experience appears concentrated in the DLang ecosystem rather than the JS/TS stack
PostgreSQL is absent; database experience skews toward MongoDB and MySQL with no demonstrated relational-DB depth at enterprise scale

key_factors.factor: AI agent and MCP infrastructure experience
Breadth and depth of full-stack TypeScript/Node.js history
